faq.heading: Questions dancers ask us.
faq.a1: The Mambo Guild is the number 1 gamified, world-class learning management system (LMS) dedicated to Salsa On2 (New York Style Mambo). Unlike static video libraries, the Guild uses Learning Experience Design (LXD) to provide a structured, interactive roadmap. Students navigate a visual Skill Tree, get feedback from instructors and peers, and learn from over 500 classes as they master everything from foundational footwork to advanced social dancing mechanics.
faq.q2: How is The Mambo Guild different from other online classes?
faq.a2: Most online dance platforms are "content graveyards": hundreds of videos with no clear path. The Guild is built on Autonomy. We replace "watch and copy" with objective biomechanics and a structured curriculum. Features like A/B Looping, Frame-by-Frame slow motion, and a community-driven Lab for technical Q&A ensure you aren't just watching dance, you're intrinsically motivated to become a dancer.
faq.q3: Who teaches The Mambo Guild courses?
faq.a3: Courses are led by Pavle Popovic, a professional Mambo instructor and competitor. The curriculum isn't just based on personal style; it integrates the history of Mambo (based on Juliet McMains' research) and modern motor-learning science. In our teaching philosophy we value logic and clear communication methods.
faq.q4: Can I learn salsa without a partner?
faq.a4: Absolutely. In fact, the Guild's philosophy is that autonomy in solo practice is the fastest way to partner-dancing mastery. Our curriculum focuses heavily on shines (solo footwork), body mechanics, timing, and weight transfer. By building a "solo" foundation of balance and coordination, you become a more reliable, fluid, and confident partner when you hit the social dance floor.
faq.q5: Does The Mambo Guild work for beginners?
faq.a5: Yes. The Skill Tree was designed specifically for the "I don't know where to start" phase. Beginners aren't left to guess; they follow a visual constellation of lessons that unlock in a logical order. With our 7-Day Free Trial, beginners can test the waters, earn their first XP, and see the roadmap to mastery before committing.

footer.tagline: The world's first structured salsa academy. We use proven methods to make you a better dancer, faster.

instructors.pageSubtitle: Learn from world-class dancers who have dedicated their lives to mastering salsa on2
instructors.bio: With 10 years of experience in salsa on2, Pavle combines his passion for dance with expertise in learning design. As a certified Learning Experience Designer (LXD), he has collaborated with leading experts to study motor pattern learning and dance training methodologies, bringing evidence-based teaching approaches to his students.
instructors.credCertBody: Certified Learning Experience Designer with a focus on motor pattern learning and dance training. Collaborates with experts to develop effective teaching methodologies.
instructors.credResearchBody: Combines 10 years of dance experience with scientific research on motor pattern learning to create structured, effective training programs that help students progress faster and more efficiently.
instructors.quote: Dancing is not just about steps — it's about connection, musicality, and expressing yourself through movement. My goal is to help you find your own voice on the dance floor.
instructors.joinTeamBody: We're always looking for passionate instructors who share our vision of making quality salsa education accessible to everyone.
instructors.guestBody: Throughout the year, we host workshops with internationally renowned salsa instructors, bringing you diverse perspectives and advanced techniques from around the world.
instructors.learnMoreBody: Check out our courses and start your journey with us today.
instructors.timotheBio: Timothé, also known as Mr. White, is a Paris-based salsa dancer with a strong background in mambo and Latin dance culture. First trained in Paris by Mouaze Konaté, he continued his training internationally with renowned instructors, and spent time in Mexico which deeply shaped his style. He performed as part of the duo Black and White Mambo across many countries, and later created and led his own team, Mambo All Stars. Today he teaches and performs in Paris, sharing both the technical and cultural sides of salsa.
instructors.timotheHistoryBody: Researched the history of salsa for many years and interviewed historical legends from the scene like Roly Maden. Wrote a book on the history of mambo and Latin dances.
instructors.timotheMamboBody: Deeply rooted in mambo with a touch of fusion and personal interpretation. Trained in Paris and Mexico, performed worldwide with Black and White Mambo, and led his own team Mambo All Stars.
instructors.timotheQuote: Salsa is more than steps. It is memory, culture, and the story of those who shaped it.
